title: Radosław Sikorski on Office Hours Podcast
summary: Radosław Sikorski (@sikorskiradek), former Foreign Minister and Defense Minister of Poland and a Fisher Family Fellow with the Future of Diplomacy Project, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about the rise of right-wing nationalism, Russia, and gastrodiplomacy.
body: Subscribe to the Office Hours Podcast iTunes | Google Play | Stitcher | Pocket Cast | TuneIn The latest from Belfer See more Radosław Sikorski on Office Hours Podcast Print Europe Europe and the Transatlantic Relationship Future of Diplomacy Project North America Related Radoslaw Sikorski More From This Expert Radosław Sikorski on Office Hours

_list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: In this episode of the Office Hours Podcast, Radosław Sikorski, a former Polish Foreign Minister and current international relations scholar, discusses the evolving dynamics of the transatlantic relationship. He analyzes the geopolitical challenges facing Europe and North America, including shifts in U.S. foreign policy, the rise of other global powers, and internal cohesion within the European Union. Sikorski offers insights into the future of diplomacy, emphasizing the need for strategic adaptation and renewed partnership to address shared security and economic concerns. The conversation explores the role of statecraft in a changing world order, highlighting the intersection of traditional diplomacy with emerging global trends.
result.main_content_zh: 在本期《Office Hours》播客节目中，前波兰外交部长、现任国际关系学者拉多斯瓦夫·西科尔斯基讨论了跨大西洋关系的演变动态。他分析了欧洲和北美面临的地缘政治挑战，包括美国外交政策的转变、其他全球大国的崛起以及欧盟内部的凝聚力问题。西科尔斯基就外交的未来提供了见解，强调需要进行战略调整并重建伙伴关系，以应对共同的安全和经济关切。对话探讨了在不断变化的世界秩序中国家方略的作用，突出了传统外交与新兴全球趋势的交汇点。
